source: Learning Rsync - Use unison: Using inotify-tools with unison for bidirectional sync

questions.q: Which toolchains give one-way versus two-way synchronization?
options: One-way = rsync + inotify-tools; two-way = inotify-tools + unison
questions.why: unison is added for the two-way case; rsync+inotify is the one-way case.

questions.q: unison's underlying dependency (which must be compiled first) is which language?
questions.why: unison is built on OCaml, so OCaml is compiled and installed first.

questions.q: For two-way synchronization, how many of the sync scripts must be running?
questions.why: Both scripts must be started for bidirectional sync.

questions.q: What triggers unison to run on each change in the demo?
options: An inotifywait monitor on the local directory piped into a `while read` loop
questions.why: The same inotifywait | while-read pattern as rsync, but running unison per event.

questions.q: What is the unison command form used in the script?
questions.why: -batch runs unison non-interactively; ssh://user@host//dir addresses the remote directory.

questions.q: What authentication does the unison demo require on both machines?
options: The rsync protocol with a password
questions.why: Both machines need password-free SSH login for the unattended two-way sync.
